zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Sophia Rugge於1517年出生在英國諾福爾可貝斯頓時，她的父親Sir Richard Rugge27歲，母親Helena Windsor25歲。她於1541年與William Hare在英國諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1607年5月4日在她的家鄉享年90歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Sophia Rugge于1517年出生在英国諾福爾可貝斯頓时，她的父亲Sir Richard Rugge27岁，母亲Helena Windsor25岁。她于1541年与William Hare在英国諾福爾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1607年5月4日在她的家乡享年90岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rugge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Elke, Hans, Helmut, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Roger  .
North German and Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a grower of rye or a baker of rye bread, from Low German, Dutch rogge ‘rye’.

variants.name: Roger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Oltrogge
meanings: North German: unflattering nickname for a farmer who sells or provides old rye, from Middle Low German olt ‘old’ + rogge ‘rye’ or just a differentiating name for an older bearer of the (frequent) name Rogge .

variants.name: Rog
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Bronislaw, Jacek, Janusz, Kazimierz, Ryszard, Zofia.
Polish: nickname from róg ‘horn’, or an occupational name for someone whose job was to blow a horn.
Dutch: from rog ‘ray’, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a ray.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Sophia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek word meaning ‘wisdom’. The Eastern cult of  St Sophia  arose as a result of misinterpretation of the phrase Hagia Sophia ‘holy wisdom’ as if it meant ‘St Sophia’. The name became popular in England in the 17th and 18th centuries—the heroine of  Fielding's  novel Tom Jones (  1749 ) is called  Sophia Weston—and  has been increasing in popularity since the 1990s. In Scotland it has been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Beathag .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Sophia Rugge was born in 1517, in Beeston, Norfolk, England, her father, Sir Richard Rugge, was 27 and her mother, Helena Windsor, was 25. She married William Hare in 1541, in Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 4 May 1607, in her hometown, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Sophia Rugge est née en 1517, à Beeston, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Rugge, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Helena Windsor, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé William Hare en 1541, à Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 mai 1607, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 90 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Sophia Rugge 1517 in Beeston, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Richard Rugge 27 und ihre Mutter Helena Windsor 25. Sie hat 1541 in Norfolk, England William Hare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. Mai 1607 im Alter von 90 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Sophia Rugge è nata il nel 1517 nel Beeston, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Sir Richard Rugge, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Helena Windsor, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato William Hare nel 1541, nel Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 4 maggio 1607, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 90 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Sophia Ruggeが1517年にイギリスノーフォルクビーストンで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Ruggeは27歳，母Helena Windsorは25歳であった。1541年にイギリスノーフォルクでWilliam Hareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1607年5月4日に故郷で90歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Sophia Rugge(이)가 1517년 영국 노르폴크 브이스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard Rugge(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helena Windsor(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1541년 영국 노르폴크에서 William Hare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1607년 5월 4일 그녀의 고향에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Sophia Rugge nasceu em 1517, em Beeston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Rugge, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Helena Windsor, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com William Hare em 1541, em Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de maio de 1607, em sua cidade natal, com 90 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Sophia Rugge родилась в 1517 г. в местности бистон, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard Rugge было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Helena Windsor, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Hare в 1541 г. в местности норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 4 мая 1607 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 90 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Sophia Rugge nació en 1517, en Beeston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Rugge, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Helena Windsor, tenía 25 años. Se casó con William Hare en 1541, en Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 4 de mayo de 1607, en su pueblo, a la edad de 90 años.
